title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 1 RECAP
content: Explosive. Week 1 of the 2026 Revolution Spring Men's Basketball League announced itself with a bang — a jaw-dropping 40-point masterclass from Hakeem "The Dream" Faud, a one-point squeaker between Purple and Blue that came down to the final possession, two 30-point scorers erupting on the same team as Black rolled past Pink, White clamping down on a furious Gray second-half charge, and Green flexing their depth against Red from wire to wire. Let's get into the action.

games.text: Yellow came out with something to prove. Alex Philo made sure everyone knew it — torching the nets for 26 points while connecting on 8-of-15 from deep. Drew Pelkey controlled the boards with authority, grabbing 11 rebounds while contributing 23 points to keep Yellow ahead. Yellow built a slim one-point lead heading into halftime while Tan leaned on Faud to stay in the fight. Halftime: Yellow 38, Tan 37. Tan was ready to flip the script in the second half, and Hakeem Faud was unstoppable. Faud took over in the final 20 minutes in historic fashion, finishing with 40 points on 19-of-33 shooting to go along with 15 rebounds, 7 assists, and 4 steals — one of the most complete individual performances in recent league history. Brody Snow contributed 12 steady points, and Devin Bradford quietly orchestrated with 6 assists and 7 rebounds to keep Tan's engine running. Tan outscored Yellow 50-46 in the second half to pull away for the win. Tan won, 87-84.

performers.Tan: Hakeem Faud — 40 pts, 15 REB, 7 AST, 4 STL; Brody Snow — 12 pts, 5/10 FG
performers.Yellow: Alex Philo — 26 pts, 8/15 3PT, 7 REB; Drew Pelkey — 23 pts, 11 REB; Logan Letourneau — 17 pts, 3 AST

games.text: One possession. That's all that separated these two teams. Blue's Matt "Swiss Army Knife" Spear put on an early-season statement performance, pouring in 32 points on 7-of-11 shooting from three while hauling in 11 rebounds to give Blue a first-half edge. James Hare ran the offense with 3 assists, and "Mean" Joe Ruggles drilled a couple of threes. Blue executed well enough to grab a 5-point halftime lead. For Purple, Trevis Buckhannon was a force in the paint from the jump — scoring and cleaning the glass relentlessly — while Jimmy Warden kept the offense steady with 14 points and 4 assists. Halftime: Blue 35, Purple 30. Purple dug deep and refused to go quietly in the second half. Buckhannon was nothing short of dominant down the stretch, finishing the night with 22 points 20 rebounds, and a couple of jaw-dropping slam dunks to anchor Purple's comeback. Peter Walsh added 16 points and 11 rebounds off the glass to give Purple a formidable interior presence, and the Purple defense tightened the screws just enough to hold Blue at bay. Spear couldn't find enough support in the closing minutes, and Purple survived the tightest of finishes when Warden went to the line, calmly sunk the front and back end of a one-and-one, and on the ensuing defensive possession, swatted any sweet dreams of a Blue buzzer beater with a clutch block. Purple won, 63-62.

performers.Purple: Trevis Buckhannon — 22 pts, 20 REB, 3 AST; Peter Walsh — 16 pts, 11 REB; Jimmy Warden — 14 pts, 7 REB, 4 AST
performers.Blue: Matt Spear — 32 pts, 7/11 3PT, 11 REB, 4 STL; Joe Ruggles — 9 pts, 4 AST; James Hare — 6 pts, 3 AST

games.text: White came with a plan and executed it early. Matt Riordan set the tone for White right out of the gate, attacking the basket and getting to the foul line repeatedly, finishing with a perfect 10-of-10 from the stripe. John Helsel was nearly automatic from the field, converting 7-of-8 attempts for 16 efficient points and even throwing down a mean one-handed jam. Meanwhile, Tavian Barrino relentlessly patrolled the boards with 12 rebounds. For Gray, Kevin Garrison put his teammates on his back — knocking down shots from all three levels and refusing to let the gap get out of hand. However, White held an 8-point edge heading into the locker room. Halftime: White 39, Gray 31. Gray rallied hard in the second half and made things interesting. Garrison continued his assault in the second half, finishing with a mighty 30 points on 5-of-15 from three and 9-of-10 from the free throw line to go along with 10 rebounds — a warrior's effort in a what ended up being a losing cause. Manny Robertson pitched in 19 points to keep the pressure on, and for a stretch, it felt like Gray might pull it off. The second half ended in a dead-even 35-35 tie, but White's first-half cushion proved to be the difference. White won, 74-66.

performers.Gray: Kevin Garrison — 30 pts, 10 REB, 4 AST; Manny Robertson — 19 pts, 3 REB
performers.White: Matt Riordan — 20 pts, 10 REB, 10/10 FT; John Helsel — 16 pts, 6 REB, 7/8 FG; Tavian Barrino — 7 pts, 12 REB, 3 AST

games.text: Black came armed with two heat-seeking missiles. Chris Cayole and Stefan Radan wasted no time putting their stamp on the 2026 season — combining for 65 points in a statement performance. Cayole knocked down 6 three-pointers and attacked the glass for 10 rebounds, while Radan buried 4 from deep and drew contact consistently, going 4-of-5 from the stripe. Tanner Freeman ran the show from the point, distributing to both scorers and keeping the offense in rhythm. Pink's Kyle Haley fought back hard, and Dennis Schut quietly hauled in 13 rebounds to keep Pink within striking distance at the half. Halftime: Black 37, Pink 32. Black pulled away decisively in the second half and never looked back. Pink's Tyrone "Big Dog" Conley played with serious heart in the second frame — finishing with 14 points, 11 rebounds, and 3 steals — and Liam Walsh contributed a double-double effort on the boards with 12 rebounds. But Black was too deep and too talented to be caught. Cayole and Radan kept pouring it on, and Freeman capped the afternoon with 8 assists as Black cruised to a comfortable opening-week victory. Black won, 78-61.

performers.Black: Chris Cayole — 33 pts, 10 REB, 6/12 3PT; Stefan Radan — 32 pts, 7 REB, 4/10 3PT; Tanner Freeman — 3 pts, 8 AST
performers.Pink: Kyle Haley — 21 pts, 5 REB; Tyrone Conley — 14 pts, 11 REB, 3 STL; Liam Walsh — 10 pts, 12 REB

games.text: Green was all business from the opening tip. Asko Mostarlic came out firing, connecting on 7-of-13 from three on his way to 24 points and 11 rebounds in a dominant all-around performance. Mahlik Franklin was equally impressive — 22 points, 6 rebounds, and 5 assists — making the right play on every possession. Andy Bousono ran the offense with veteran poise, distributing with 6 assists and keeping Green's attack varied and unpredictable. Red's Jibril Abdullahi was Green's toughest assignment, battling for 20 points to keep Red within earshot at the half. Halftime: Green 34, Red 30. Green pulled the hammer down in the second half and put it out of reach. Any flicker of hope Red had was quickly extinguished as Green dominated the second frame 41-25. Kerry Lyons brought the blue-collar energy that winning teams need — 11 points and 9 rebounds to complement Green's star performers. Josh Carter continued to compete hard for Red with 12 points, but Green's balanced attack had an answer for everything Red threw at them. Green won, 75-55.

performers.Green: Asko Mostarlic — 24 pts, 11 REB, 7/13 3PT; Mahlik Franklin — 22 pts, 6 REB, 5 AST; Kerry Lyons — 11 pts, 9 REB
performers.Red: Jibril Abdullahi — 20 pts, 5 REB; Josh Carter — 12 pts, 3 REB, 2 STL; Logan Tobin — 10 pts, 5/10 FG

closing: Week 1 is in the books, and the 2026 Revolution Spring League is off to a spectacular start. Tune in tomorrow as the standings begin to take shape — you won't want to miss the action!
